prevalence.trend: Increasing by 2-3% annually due to rising diabetes and hypertension

burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study (IHME) adapted for Brazil

vulnerablePopulations: Patients with uncontrolled diabetes or hypertension
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: eGFR <60 mL/min/1.73m² for >3 months or markers of kidney damage (albuminuria); classified by KDIGO stages 1-5

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Approximately 15 million adults affected, 120,000 on dialysis
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 45% for early stages; 70% access to dialysis/ transplant via SUS

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Follows KDIGO guidelines, with emphasis on eGFR and albuminuria testing in primary care
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Multidisciplinary approach via SUS: ACE inhibitors/ARBs for early stages, dialysis or transplant for ESRD; protocols from SBN
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Disparities in access between urban (South/Southeast) and rural/North/Northeast regions; high costs for dialysis in remote areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust SUS coverage for dialysis (universal access); national registries like Caderno de Informações de Saúde da Atenção Básica (CISAB) for monitoring
